entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The target crop of the study, Arachis hypogaea L.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and productivity under water-limited conditions.

entities.name: WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to produce biomass per unit of water used.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A physiological trait associated with drought tolerance, for which SLA and SCMR serve as surrogates.

entities.name: SPECIFIC LEAF AREA
entities.description: A physiological trait used as a surrogate for transpiration efficiency, measured as the ratio of leaf area to dry mass.

entities.name: SPAD CHLOROPHYLL METER READING
entities.description: A non-invasive measurement of chlorophyll content used as a surrogate for transpiration efficiency.

entities.name: MOISTURE DEFICIT STRESS
entities.description: A condition of limited soil moisture used to evaluate plant physiological responses.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: ICGV 99029
entities.description: A groundnut genotype identified as a good parent for water-use efficiency.

entities.name: ICR 48
entities.description: A groundnut genotype identified as a good parent for water-use efficiency.

entities.name: ICGS 76
entities.description: A groundnut genotype identified as a good parent for water-use efficiency.

entities.name: TCGS 647
entities.description: A groundnut genotype identified as a good parent for water-use efficiency.

entities.name: TCGP 6
entities.description: A groundnut genotype identified as a good parent for water-use efficiency.

entities.name: BREEDING AND SELECTION
entities.description: The process of developing improved crop genotypes through genetic selection.
